title: Cohere releases North Mini Code, an open developer model for coding agents
friendlyTitle: Cohere released an open coding model
summary: Cohere released North Mini Code, a 30B-parameter mixture-of-experts coding model with 3B active parameters, Apache 2.0 licensing, Hugging Face weights, Cohere API access, and training focused on agentic software engineering tasks.
friendlySummary: Cohere released North Mini Code, an open model trained for coding-agent work and available through Hugging Face and Cohere.
whyItMatters: Open coding models give teams more options for private or lower-cost software agents. The release also reflects a broader move toward models trained for terminal work and multi-step repository tasks, not just code completion.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Teams get more options for private or lower-cost coding agents that can work through multi-step software tasks.
